Spatial Inequalities in Street-Tree Ecological Quality across New York City¶

This notebook implements the full data processing and analysis pipeline for the midterm submission.

Research Questions:

  • RQ1: What spatial inequalities and clustering patterns characterize street-tree health, structural attributes, and species diversity across NYC?
  • RQ2: How do street-tree ecological quality indicators associate with socioeconomic vulnerability, built-environment intensity (including 2D urban morphology and landscape configuration), and green infrastructure supply?
  • RQ3: Where do multiple disadvantages (low tree quality + high social vulnerability + high built density / fragmented greenery) overlap, and can we identify priority intervention areas?

0. Setup¶

In [3]:
import os
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import rasterio
from rasterio.mask import mask as rio_mask
from rasterstats import zonal_stats
from scipy.stats import entropy, pearsonr
from scipy.ndimage import label, binary_dilation
from shapely.geometry import mapping
from shapely import wkt
import missingno as msno
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Paths (relative to notebooks/)
RAW = '../data/raw/'
PROCESSED = '../data/processed/'
VIS = '../visualizations/'
os.makedirs(PROCESSED, exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs(VIS, exist_ok=True)

plt.rcParams.update({
    'figure.dpi': 150,
    'font.family': 'sans-serif',
    'font.size': 10,
    'axes.titlesize': 12,
    'axes.labelsize': 10,
})

# Borough color palette (used across all figures)
BOROUGH_COLORS = {
    'Manhattan': '#e41a1c', 'Brooklyn': '#377eb8', 'Queens': '#4daf4a',
    'Bronx': '#ff7f00', 'Staten Island': '#984ea3'
}

print('All imports successful.')

All imports successful.

1. Census Tract Boundaries¶

Data source: 2020 TIGER/Line Census Tracts for New York State, downloaded from the U.S. Census Bureau.
Coverage: Five NYC counties (FIPS 005=Bronx, 047=Brooklyn, 061=Manhattan, 081=Queens, 085=Staten Island).
Granularity: Polygon; one record per Census Tract.
Format: Shapefile (.shp).
Source: https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/TIGER2020/TRACT/

Census Tracts serve as the primary spatial unit because they (1) align directly with ACS socioeconomic estimates, (2) are the standard unit for environmental justice analyses, and (3) provide sufficient spatial resolution (~2,300 tracts in NYC).

In [4]:
tracts_all = gpd.read_file(RAW + 'census_tracts/tl_2020_36_tract.shp')
nyc_fips = ['005', '047', '061', '081', '085']
tracts = tracts_all[tracts_all['COUNTYFP'].isin(nyc_fips)].copy()
tracts = tracts.to_crs(epsg=26918)  # UTM 18N for accurate area calculations
tracts['area_m2'] = tracts.geometry.area
tracts['area_km2'] = tracts['area_m2'] / 1e6

borough_map = {'005': 'Bronx', '047': 'Brooklyn', '061': 'Manhattan',
               '081': 'Queens', '085': 'Staten Island'}
tracts['borough'] = tracts['COUNTYFP'].map(borough_map)

tracts.to_file(PROCESSED + 'nyc_tracts.gpkg', driver='GPKG')
print(f'NYC Census Tracts: {len(tracts)}')
print(tracts['borough'].value_counts().to_string())

NYC Census Tracts: 2327
borough
Brooklyn         805
Queens           725
Bronx            361
Manhattan        310
Staten Island    126

2. Street Tree Census — Cleaning and Tract-Level Aggregation¶

Data source: TreesCount! 2015–2016 Street Tree Census from NYC Open Data.
Coverage: All five NYC boroughs; collected 2015–2016.
Granularity: Point; one record per individual street tree (683,788 raw records).
Type: Quantitative (DBH) and categorical (health status, species).
Format: CSV with latitude/longitude coordinates.
Source: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Environment/2015-Street-Tree-Census-Tree-Data/uvpi-gqnh

Cleaning steps:

  1. Filter to status == "Alive" (exclude Dead and Stump records)
  2. Remove records with missing or zero coordinates
  3. Cap DBH outliers (> 100 inches set to NaN)
  4. Encode health numerically: Good=3, Fair=2, Poor=1
  5. Spatial join to Census Tracts
  6. Aggregate to tract level: count, density, mean DBH, health index, Shannon diversity
In [5]:
trees_raw = pd.read_csv(RAW + 'tree_census_2015.csv', low_memory=False)
print(f'Raw records: {len(trees_raw):,}')
print(f'\nStatus distribution:\n{trees_raw["status"].value_counts().to_string()}')
print(f'\nHealth distribution (Alive only):')
print(trees_raw[trees_raw['status'] == 'Alive']['health'].value_counts().to_string())

Raw records: 683,788

Status distribution:
status
Alive    652173
Stump     17654
Dead      13961

Health distribution (Alive only):
health
Good    528850
Fair     96504
Poor     26818
In [6]:
# Step 1: Filter to alive trees
trees = trees_raw[trees_raw['status'] == 'Alive'].copy()
print(f'After alive filter: {len(trees):,} ({len(trees)/len(trees_raw)*100:.1f}%)')

# Step 2: Remove missing/zero coordinates
before = len(trees)
trees = trees.dropna(subset=['latitude', 'longitude'])
trees = trees[(trees['latitude'] != 0) & (trees['longitude'] != 0)]
print(f'After coordinate cleaning: {len(trees):,} (removed {before - len(trees)})')

# Step 3: Cap DBH outliers
outlier_mask = trees['tree_dbh'] > 100
print(f'DBH outliers (>100 in): {outlier_mask.sum()}')
trees.loc[outlier_mask, 'tree_dbh'] = np.nan

# Step 4: Encode health numerically
health_map = {'Good': 3, 'Fair': 2, 'Poor': 1}
trees['health_score'] = trees['health'].map(health_map)
print(f'Health score mapped: {trees["health_score"].notna().sum():,} valid')
print(f'\nCleaned dataset: {len(trees):,} alive street trees')

After alive filter: 652,173 (95.4%)
After coordinate cleaning: 652,173 (removed 0)
DBH outliers (>100 in): 67
Health score mapped: 652,172 valid

Cleaned dataset: 652,173 alive street trees
In [7]:
# Step 5: Convert to GeoDataFrame and spatial join to tracts
trees_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    trees,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(trees['longitude'], trees['latitude']),
    crs='EPSG:4326'
).to_crs(epsg=26918)

trees_joined = gpd.sjoin(
    trees_gdf, tracts[['GEOID', 'borough', 'geometry']],
    how='inner', predicate='within'
)
print(f'Trees joined to tracts: {len(trees_joined):,}')

# Rename borough column if spatial join created suffix
if 'borough_left' in trees_joined.columns:
    trees_joined = trees_joined.drop(columns=['borough_left'], errors='ignore')
if 'borough_right' in trees_joined.columns:
    trees_joined = trees_joined.rename(columns={'borough_right': 'borough_sj'})
# Ensure a clean 'borough' column from the join
if 'borough_sj' in trees_joined.columns and 'borough' not in trees_joined.columns:
    trees_joined = trees_joined.rename(columns={'borough_sj': 'borough'})

trees_joined.drop(columns=['geometry']).to_csv(PROCESSED + 'trees_cleaned.csv', index=False)
print('Saved trees_cleaned.csv')

Trees joined to tracts: 652,045
Saved trees_cleaned.csv
In [8]:
# Step 6: Aggregate to tract level
def shannon_diversity(species_series):
    """Compute Shannon diversity index H' from a series of species names."""
    counts = species_series.value_counts()
    proportions = counts / counts.sum()
    return entropy(proportions, base=np.e)

# Determine which borough column to use
boro_col = 'borough' if 'borough' in trees_joined.columns else 'borough_right'

tract_trees = trees_joined.groupby('GEOID').agg(
    tree_count=('tree_id', 'count'),
    mean_dbh=('tree_dbh', 'mean'),
    median_dbh=('tree_dbh', 'median'),
    health_index=('health_score', 'mean'),
    pct_good=('health', lambda x: (x == 'Good').mean() * 100),
    pct_fair=('health', lambda x: (x == 'Fair').mean() * 100),
    pct_poor=('health', lambda x: (x == 'Poor').mean() * 100),
    species_count=('spc_latin', 'nunique'),
    species_diversity=('spc_latin', shannon_diversity),
    dominant_species=('spc_common', lambda x: x.value_counts().index[0] if len(x) > 0 else np.nan),
).reset_index()

tract_trees = tract_trees.merge(tracts[['GEOID', 'area_km2']], on='GEOID')
tract_trees['tree_density'] = tract_trees['tree_count'] / tract_trees['area_km2']

tract_trees.to_csv(PROCESSED + 'trees_by_tract.csv', index=False)
print(f'Tract-level tree indicators: {len(tract_trees)} tracts')
tract_trees[['tree_count', 'mean_dbh', 'health_index', 'species_diversity', 'tree_density']].describe().round(2)

Tract-level tree indicators: 2306 tracts

Dominant Species Ecological Profile¶

NYC's top 15 street tree species and their ecological characteristics, compiled from USDA plant databases and urban forestry literature.

In [9]:
top_species = trees_joined['spc_common'].value_counts().head(15)
print('Top 15 street tree species in NYC:')
for i, (sp, cnt) in enumerate(top_species.items(), 1):
    pct = cnt / len(trees_joined) * 100
    print(f'  {i:2d}. {sp:<30s} {cnt:>7,}  ({pct:.1f}%)')

species_profiles = {
    'London planetree':  {'shade': 'High',     'heat_tol': 'High',     'pollution_tol': 'High',     'stormwater': 'High',     'native': False},
    'honeylocust':       {'shade': 'Moderate', 'heat_tol': 'High',     'pollution_tol': 'High',     'stormwater': 'Moderate', 'native': True},
    'Callery pear':      {'shade': 'Moderate', 'heat_tol': 'Moderate', 'pollution_tol': 'High',     'stormwater': 'Low',      'native': False},
    'pin oak':           {'shade': 'High',     'heat_tol': 'Moderate', 'pollution_tol': 'Moderate', 'stormwater': 'High',     'native': True},

}
sp_df = pd.DataFrame(species_profiles).T
sp_df.index.name = 'species'
sp_df.to_csv(PROCESSED + 'species_profiles.csv')

3. UrbanWatch Land Cover — Classification and Zonal Statistics¶

Data source: UrbanWatch 1-meter resolution urban land cover classification (University of Vermont / UNC Charlotte).
Coverage: New York City extent.
Granularity: Raster; 1 m² per pixel; 42,000 × 46,674 pixels.
Type: Categorical (9 land cover classes encoded as RGB triplets).
Format: GeoTIFF, 3-band RGB, EPSG:26918.
Source: https://urbanwatch.charlotte.edu/
Citation: Zhang et al. (2022). Remote Sensing of Environment, 278, 113106.

Processing:

  1. Decode 3-band RGB into a single-band classified raster (9 classes + nodata)
  2. Compute per-tract zonal statistics: proportion of each land cover class (PLAND)
  3. Derive composite indicators: impervious ratio, pervious ratio
In [10]:
# UrbanWatch RGB-to-class mapping
UW_CLASS_MAP = {
    (255,   0,   0): 1,   # Building
    (133, 133, 133): 2,   # Road
    (255,   0, 192): 3,   # Parking lot
    ( 34, 139,  34): 4,   # Tree canopy
    (128, 236, 104): 5,   # Grass/shrub
    (  0,   0, 255): 6,   # Water
    (128,   0,   0): 7,   # Agriculture
    (255, 255, 255): 8,   # Barren
}
UW_CLASS_NAMES = {
    1: 'building', 2: 'road', 3: 'parking', 4: 'tree_canopy',
    5: 'grass_shrub', 6: 'water', 7: 'agriculture', 8: 'barren'
}
print('UrbanWatch class mapping:')
for rgb, cls_id in UW_CLASS_MAP.items():
    print(f'  RGB{rgb} -> {cls_id}: {UW_CLASS_NAMES[cls_id]}')

UrbanWatch class mapping:
  RGB(255, 0, 0) -> 1: building
  RGB(133, 133, 133) -> 2: road
  RGB(255, 0, 192) -> 3: parking
  RGB(34, 139, 34) -> 4: tree_canopy
  RGB(128, 236, 104) -> 5: grass_shrub
  RGB(0, 0, 255) -> 6: water
  RGB(128, 0, 0) -> 7: agriculture
  RGB(255, 255, 255) -> 8: barren
In [11]:
# Convert 3-band RGB raster to single-band classified raster
classified_path = PROCESSED + 'urbanwatch_classified.tif'

if not os.path.exists(classified_path):
    print('Converting RGB raster to classified (this takes ~1 minute)...')
    with rasterio.open(RAW + 'NYC_urbanwatch_merged.tif') as src:
        r, g, b = src.read(1), src.read(2), src.read(3)
        classified = np.zeros(r.shape, dtype=np.uint8)
        for (rv, gv, bv), cls_id in UW_CLASS_MAP.items():
            mask = (r == rv) & (g == gv) & (b == bv)
            classified[mask] = cls_id
            print(f'  Class {cls_id} ({UW_CLASS_NAMES[cls_id]}): {mask.sum():,} pixels')
        profile = src.profile.copy()
        profile.update(count=1, dtype='uint8', nodata=0)
        with rasterio.open(classified_path, 'w', **profile) as dst:
            dst.write(classified, 1)
    total_valid = (classified > 0).sum()
    print(f'\nTotal valid pixels: {total_valid:,}')
    print('Classified raster saved.')
else:
    print(f'Classified raster already exists: {classified_path}')

Classified raster already exists: ../data/processed/urbanwatch_classified.tif
In [12]:
# Zonal statistics: count of each class per tract
tracts_utm = tracts.to_crs(epsg=26918)

print('Computing zonal statistics (takes ~2 minutes)...')
lc_stats = zonal_stats(tracts_utm, classified_path, categorical=True, nodata=0)
lc_df = pd.DataFrame(lc_stats).fillna(0)
lc_df['GEOID'] = tracts_utm['GEOID'].values

# Sum only valid class columns (integers > 0) for total pixel count
class_cols = [c for c in lc_df.columns if isinstance(c, (int, float)) and c > 0]
lc_df['total_pixels'] = lc_df[class_cols].sum(axis=1)

# Compute PLAND for each class
for cls_id, cls_name in UW_CLASS_NAMES.items():
    if cls_id in lc_df.columns:
        lc_df[f'pland_{cls_name}'] = lc_df[cls_id] / lc_df['total_pixels'] * 100
    else:
        lc_df[f'pland_{cls_name}'] = 0.0

# Composite indicators
lc_df['pland_impervious'] = lc_df['pland_building'] + lc_df['pland_road'] + lc_df['pland_parking']
lc_df['pland_pervious'] = lc_df['pland_tree_canopy'] + lc_df['pland_grass_shrub'] + lc_df['pland_water'] + lc_df['pland_agriculture']

pland_cols = [c for c in lc_df.columns if isinstance(c, str) and c.startswith('pland_')]
tract_lc = lc_df[['GEOID'] + pland_cols].copy()
tract_lc.to_csv(PROCESSED + 'landcover_by_tract.csv', index=False)
print(f'Land cover saved: {len(tract_lc)} tracts, {len(pland_cols)} indicators')
tract_lc[pland_cols].describe().round(2)

Computing zonal statistics (takes ~2 minutes)...
Land cover saved: 2327 tracts, 10 indicators

4. NYC Building Footprints — Tract-Level Aggregation¶

Data source: NYC Building Footprints (NYC DoITT / Open Data).
Coverage: All five boroughs; ~1.08 million buildings.
Granularity: Polygon; one record per building footprint.
Type: Quantitative (height_roof in feet).
Format: GeoPackage (.gpkg), EPSG:4326.
Source: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Housing-Development/Building-Footprints/nqwf-w8eh

Processing:

  1. Reproject to UTM 18N; compute footprint area from geometry
  2. Convert height_roof from feet to meters
  3. Spatial join to Census Tracts via representative points
  4. Aggregate: mean/median/max height, building count, coverage ratio, density
In [13]:
print('Loading building footprints (~1 minute)...')
buildings = gpd.read_file(RAW + 'NYC_buildings.gpkg')
print(f'Raw buildings: {len(buildings):,}')
print(f'CRS: {buildings.crs}')
print(f'\nheight_roof stats (feet):\n{buildings["height_roof"].describe().to_string()}')

Loading building footprints (~1 minute)...
Raw buildings: 1,082,289
CRS: EPSG:4326

height_roof stats (feet):
count    1.082289e+06
mean     2.779539e+01
std      2.231814e+01
min      0.000000e+00
25%      1.855000e+01

75%      3.118000e+01

In [14]:
buildings = buildings.to_crs(epsg=26918)
buildings['footprint_m2'] = buildings.geometry.area
buildings['height_m'] = buildings['height_roof'] * 0.3048  # feet to meters

# Spatial join via representative points to avoid boundary issues
print('Spatial joining buildings to tracts...')
buildings['rep_point'] = buildings.geometry.representative_point()
bldg_points = buildings.set_geometry('rep_point')
bldg_joined = gpd.sjoin(
    bldg_points, tracts_utm[['GEOID', 'area_m2', 'geometry']],
    how='inner', predicate='within'
)
print(f'Buildings joined: {len(bldg_joined):,}')

tract_bldg = bldg_joined.groupby('GEOID').agg(
    building_count=('height_m', 'count'),
    mean_height_m=('height_m', 'mean'),
    median_height_m=('height_m', 'median'),
    max_height_m=('height_m', 'max'),
    total_footprint_m2=('footprint_m2', 'sum'),
    tract_area_m2=('area_m2', 'first'),
).reset_index()
tract_bldg['building_coverage'] = tract_bldg['total_footprint_m2'] / tract_bldg['tract_area_m2'] * 100
tract_bldg['building_density'] = tract_bldg['building_count'] / (tract_bldg['tract_area_m2'] / 1e6)
tract_bldg = tract_bldg.drop(columns=['tract_area_m2'])

tract_bldg.to_csv(PROCESSED + 'buildings_by_tract.csv', index=False)
print(f'Building indicators saved: {len(tract_bldg)} tracts')
tract_bldg.describe().round(2)

Spatial joining buildings to tracts...
Buildings joined: 1,082,092
Building indicators saved: 2322 tracts

5. PLUTO — Built Environment Indicators¶

Data source: Primary Land Use Tax Lot Output (PLUTO) v25.4, NYC Department of City Planning.
Coverage: All five boroughs; 858,644 tax lots.
Granularity: Tabular; one record per tax lot with built-in Census Tract identifier (bct2020).
Type: Mixed (quantitative: FAR, floors, lot area; categorical: land use).
Format: CSV.
Source: https://www.nyc.gov/site/planning/data-maps/open-data/dwn-pluto-mappluto.page

Processing:

  1. Construct 11-digit GEOID from borocode + bct2020
  2. Aggregate to tract level: mean FAR, mean floors, median year built, dominant land use
In [15]:
pluto_cols = ['borough', 'block', 'lot', 'bct2020', 'borocode', 'landuse',
              'lotarea', 'bldgarea', 'numfloors', 'yearbuilt', 'builtfar',
              'unitsres', 'unitstotal', 'assesstot', 'latitude', 'longitude']
pluto = pd.read_csv(RAW + 'pluto_25v4.csv', usecols=pluto_cols, low_memory=False)
print(f'Raw PLUTO lots: {len(pluto):,}')

# Construct GEOID: state(36) + county(3) + tract(6)
boro_to_county = {1: '061', 2: '005', 3: '047', 4: '081', 5: '085'}
pluto = pluto.dropna(subset=['bct2020', 'borocode'])
pluto['borocode'] = pluto['borocode'].astype(int)
pluto['county_fips'] = pluto['borocode'].map(boro_to_county)
pluto['tract_code'] = pluto['bct2020'].astype(int).astype(str).str[1:]  # remove borocode prefix
pluto['GEOID'] = '36' + pluto['county_fips'] + pluto['tract_code'].str.zfill(6)

tract_pluto = pluto.groupby('GEOID').agg(
    mean_far=('builtfar', 'mean'),
    mean_floors=('numfloors', 'mean'),
    median_yearbuilt=('yearbuilt', lambda x: x[x > 0].median()),
    total_lotarea=('lotarea', 'sum'),
    total_bldgarea=('bldgarea', 'sum'),
    total_res_units=('unitsres', 'sum'),
    lot_count=('lotarea', 'count'),
    dominant_landuse=('landuse', lambda x: str(int(x.mode().iloc[0])).zfill(2) if len(x.mode()) > 0 else np.nan),
).reset_index()
tract_pluto['bldg_to_lot_ratio'] = tract_pluto['total_bldgarea'] / tract_pluto['total_lotarea'].replace(0, np.nan)

tract_pluto.to_csv(PROCESSED + 'pluto_by_tract.csv', index=False)
print(f'PLUTO indicators saved: {len(tract_pluto)} tracts')
tract_pluto[['mean_far', 'mean_floors', 'median_yearbuilt', 'bldg_to_lot_ratio']].describe().round(2)

Raw PLUTO lots: 858,644
PLUTO indicators saved: 2329 tracts

6. NYC Parks Properties — Green Space Supply¶

Data source: NYC Parks Properties, NYC Department of Parks & Recreation.
Coverage: All five boroughs; 2,058 park property polygons.
Granularity: Polygon; one record per park property.
Format: CSV with WKT multipolygon geometry column.
Source: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Recreation/Parks-Properties/enfh-gkve

Processing:

  1. Parse WKT geometry from multipolygon column
  2. Intersect with Census Tracts
  3. Compute park area ratio (park area / tract area) per tract
In [16]:
parks_raw = pd.read_csv(RAW + 'nyc_parks.csv', low_memory=False)
print(f'Parks records: {len(parks_raw)}')

# Identify and parse geometry column
geom_cols = [c for c in parks_raw.columns if 'multipolygon' in c.lower() or 'polygon' in c.lower() or 'geom' in c.lower()]
print(f'Geometry columns found: {geom_cols}')

geom_col = geom_cols[0]  # 'multipolygon'
parks_raw['geometry'] = parks_raw[geom_col].apply(lambda x: wkt.loads(x) if pd.notna(x) else None)
parks_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(parks_raw, geometry='geometry', crs='EPSG:4326')
parks_gdf = parks_gdf[parks_gdf.geometry.notna()].to_crs(epsg=26918)
print(f'Parsed {len(parks_gdf)} park geometries')

# Intersect with tracts
parks_overlay = gpd.overlay(
    parks_gdf[['geometry']], tracts_utm[['GEOID', 'geometry', 'area_m2']],
    how='intersection'
)
parks_overlay['intersect_area'] = parks_overlay.geometry.area

tract_parks = parks_overlay.groupby('GEOID').agg(
    park_area_m2=('intersect_area', 'sum'),
    park_count=('intersect_area', 'count'),
).reset_index()
tract_parks = tract_parks.merge(tracts[['GEOID', 'area_m2']], on='GEOID')
tract_parks['park_area_pct'] = tract_parks['park_area_m2'] / tract_parks['area_m2'] * 100
tract_parks = tract_parks.drop(columns=['area_m2'])

tract_parks.to_csv(PROCESSED + 'parks_by_tract.csv', index=False)
print(f'Parks saved: {len(tract_parks)} tracts with park coverage')
tract_parks.describe().round(2)

Parks records: 2058
Geometry columns found: ['multipolygon']
Parsed 2058 park geometries
Parks saved: 1354 tracts with park coverage

7. ACS & SVI — Socioeconomic Indicators¶

Data sources:

  • ACS 5-Year Estimates (2019): Tract-level socioeconomic variables via Census Bureau API.
    Variables: B19013 (median income), B17001 (poverty), B02001 (race), B25003 (tenure).
    Source: https://api.census.gov/data/2019/acs/acs5
  • CDC/ATSDR Social Vulnerability Index (SVI): Composite percentile ranking on 16 social factors across four themes.
    Source: https://www.atsdr.cdc.gov/placeandhealth/svi/
In [17]:
# ACS 5-Year 2019
acs = pd.read_csv(RAW + 'acs_2019_5yr_nyc.csv', dtype=str)
acs['GEOID'] = '36' + acs['county'].str.zfill(3) + acs['tract'].str.zfill(6)

for col in ['B19013_001E', 'B17001_001E', 'B17001_002E',
            'B02001_001E', 'B02001_002E', 'B02001_003E', 'B02001_005E',
            'B25003_001E', 'B25003_002E', 'B25003_003E']:
    acs[col] = pd.to_numeric(acs[col], errors='coerce')

tract_acs = pd.DataFrame({
    'GEOID': acs['GEOID'],
    'median_income': acs['B19013_001E'],
    'pct_poverty': acs['B17001_002E'] / acs['B17001_001E'].replace(0, np.nan) * 100,
    'pct_white': acs['B02001_002E'] / acs['B02001_001E'].replace(0, np.nan) * 100,
    'pct_black': acs['B02001_003E'] / acs['B02001_001E'].replace(0, np.nan) * 100,
    'pct_asian': acs['B02001_005E'] / acs['B02001_001E'].replace(0, np.nan) * 100,
    'pct_minority': (1 - acs['B02001_002E'] / acs['B02001_001E'].replace(0, np.nan)) * 100,
    'pct_renter': acs['B25003_003E'] / acs['B25003_001E'].replace(0, np.nan) * 100,
    'total_pop': acs['B02001_001E'],
})
# Census uses -666666666 as missing income indicator
tract_acs.loc[tract_acs['median_income'] < 0, 'median_income'] = np.nan
print(f'ACS indicators: {len(tract_acs)} tracts')

# SVI
svi = pd.read_csv(RAW + 'nyc_svi.csv', low_memory=False)
svi['GEOID'] = svi['FIPS'].astype(str).str.zfill(11)
rpl_cols = [c for c in svi.columns if 'RPL_THEME' in c.upper()]
svi_select = svi[['GEOID'] + rpl_cols].copy()
for c in rpl_cols:
    svi_select[c] = pd.to_numeric(svi_select[c], errors='coerce')
    svi_select.loc[svi_select[c] < 0, c] = np.nan  # recode -999

# Merge ACS + SVI
tract_socio = tract_acs.merge(svi_select, on='GEOID', how='left')
tract_socio.to_csv(PROCESSED + 'socioeconomic_by_tract.csv', index=False)
print(f'Socioeconomic indicators saved: {len(tract_socio)} tracts')
tract_socio.describe().round(1)

Socioeconomic indicators saved: 2167 tracts

8. Landscape Pattern Metrics (FRAGSTATS-style)¶

Beyond land cover composition (PLAND), we compute landscape configuration metrics to capture the spatial arrangement and fragmentation of urban land cover.

Landscape-level (via pylandstats): SHDI (Shannon diversity), CONTAG (contagion).
Class-level (for tree canopy and building, via scipy.ndimage): NP, PD, LPI, mean patch size, ED, AI.

In [18]:
# Check for pylandstats (landscape-level metrics)
try:
    import pylandstats as pls
    HAS_PLS = True
    print('pylandstats available for landscape-level metrics')
except ImportError:
    HAS_PLS = False
    print('pylandstats not installed; landscape-level metrics will be skipped')

pylandstats not installed; landscape-level metrics will be skipped
In [19]:
def compute_tract_landscape_metrics(tract_geoid, tract_geom, raster_path):
    """Compute landscape + class-level metrics for a single tract."""
    result = {'GEOID': str(tract_geoid)}
    try:
        with rasterio.open(raster_path) as src:
            clipped, transform = rio_mask(src, [mapping(tract_geom)], crop=True, nodata=0)
            arr = clipped[0]
            total_valid = (arr > 0).sum()
            if total_valid < 100:
                return result
            total_area_ha = total_valid / 1e4  # 1m pixels -> hectares

            # Landscape-level via pylandstats
            if HAS_PLS:
                ls = pls.Landscape(arr, res=(1, 1), nodata=0)
                try: result['shdi'] = ls.shannon_diversity_index()
                except: pass
                try: result['contag'] = ls.contagion()
                except: pass

            # Class-level via scipy (tree canopy=4, building=1)
            for cls_id, cls_name in [(4, 'tree'), (1, 'bldg')]:
                binary = (arr == cls_id).astype(np.uint8)
                cls_pixels = binary.sum()
                if cls_pixels == 0:
                    continue
                labeled_arr, n_patches = label(binary)
                patch_sizes = np.bincount(labeled_arr.ravel())[1:]

                result[f'{cls_name}_np'] = int(n_patches)
                result[f'{cls_name}_pd'] = round(n_patches / total_area_ha, 4)
                result[f'{cls_name}_lpi'] = round(float(patch_sizes.max()) / total_valid * 100, 4)
                result[f'{cls_name}_mean_patch'] = round(float(patch_sizes.mean()), 2)

                # Edge density: count class/non-class transitions
                edge_count = 0
                for di, dj in [(0, 1), (1, 0)]:
                    shifted = np.roll(binary, di, axis=0) if di else np.roll(binary, dj, axis=1)
                    edge_count += np.sum(binary != shifted)
                result[f'{cls_name}_ed'] = round(edge_count / total_area_ha, 2)

                # Aggregation index: ratio of like-adjacencies
                like_adj = 0
                for di, dj in [(0, 1), (1, 0)]:
                    shifted = np.roll(binary, di, axis=0) if di else np.roll(binary, dj, axis=1)
                    like_adj += np.sum((binary == 1) & (shifted == 1))
                result[f'{cls_name}_ai'] = round(like_adj / max(cls_pixels, 1) * 100, 2)
    except:
        pass
    return result

print('Landscape metric function defined.')

Landscape metric function defined.
In [20]:
fragstats_path = PROCESSED + 'fragstats_by_tract.csv'

if not os.path.exists(fragstats_path):
    print(f'Computing landscape metrics for {len(tracts_utm)} tracts (~5 minutes)...')
    results = []
    for i, row in tracts_utm.iterrows():
        res = compute_tract_landscape_metrics(row['GEOID'], row['geometry'], classified_path)
        results.append(res)
        if len(results) % 500 == 0:
            print(f'  {len(results)}/{len(tracts_utm)} done...')
    tract_frag = pd.DataFrame(results)
    tract_frag.to_csv(fragstats_path, index=False)
    print(f'Landscape metrics saved: {len(tract_frag)} tracts')
else:
    tract_frag = pd.read_csv(fragstats_path)
    tract_frag['GEOID'] = tract_frag['GEOID'].astype(str).str.zfill(11)
    print(f'Loaded existing fragstats: {len(tract_frag)} tracts')

metric_cols = [c for c in tract_frag.columns if c != 'GEOID']
print(f'Metrics ({len(metric_cols)}): {metric_cols}')
tract_frag[metric_cols].describe().round(2)

9. Merge All Indicators into Master Table¶

Join all tract-level indicators using GEOID as the common key.

In [21]:
master = tracts[['GEOID', 'NAMELSAD', 'borough', 'area_km2', 'area_m2', 'geometry']].copy()

datasets = {
    'trees':    pd.read_csv(PROCESSED + 'trees_by_tract.csv'),
    'buildings': pd.read_csv(PROCESSED + 'buildings_by_tract.csv'),
    'landcover': pd.read_csv(PROCESSED + 'landcover_by_tract.csv'),
    'pluto':    pd.read_csv(PROCESSED + 'pluto_by_tract.csv'),
    'parks':    pd.read_csv(PROCESSED + 'parks_by_tract.csv'),
    'socio':    pd.read_csv(PROCESSED + 'socioeconomic_by_tract.csv'),
    'fragstats': pd.read_csv(PROCESSED + 'fragstats_by_tract.csv'),
}

for name, df in datasets.items():
    df['GEOID'] = df['GEOID'].astype(str).str.zfill(11)
    drop_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c in master.columns and c != 'GEOID']
    df = df.drop(columns=drop_cols, errors='ignore')
    before = master.shape[1]
    master = master.merge(df, on='GEOID', how='left')
    added = master.shape[1] - before
    matched = df.merge(master[['GEOID']], on='GEOID', how='inner').shape[0]
    print(f'  {name}: +{added} cols, {matched}/{len(master)} matched')

# Post-merge cleaning
for col in ['tree_count', 'tree_density', 'park_area_pct', 'park_area_m2', 'park_count']:
    if col in master.columns:
        master[col] = master[col].fillna(0)

print(f'\nMaster table: {master.shape[0]} tracts x {master.shape[1]} columns')

master.drop(columns=['geometry']).to_csv(PROCESSED + 'tract_indicators_full.csv', index=False)
master.to_file(PROCESSED + 'tract_indicators_full.gpkg', driver='GPKG')
print('Saved tract_indicators_full.csv and .gpkg')

  trees: +11 cols, 2306/2327 matched
  buildings: +7 cols, 2322/2327 matched
  landcover: +10 cols, 2327/2327 matched
  pluto: +9 cols, 2322/2327 matched
  parks: +3 cols, 1354/2327 matched
  socio: +13 cols, 2036/2327 matched
  fragstats: +14 cols, 2327/2327 matched

Master table: 2327 tracts x 73 columns
Saved tract_indicators_full.csv and .gpkg

10. Missing Data Analysis¶

We systematically assess missing values across all variables, classify them according to the Dark Data taxonomy (Hand, 2020), and document our handling strategy.

In [22]:
numeric_master = master.select_dtypes(include=[np.number])
missing_pct = (numeric_master.isna().sum() / len(numeric_master) * 100).sort_values(ascending=False)
missing_pct = missing_pct[missing_pct > 0]

print('Variables with missing data:')
print('=' * 60)
for var, pct in missing_pct.items():
    n_miss = numeric_master[var].isna().sum()
    print(f'  {var:<35s} {n_miss:>5d} missing  ({pct:5.1f}%)')

In [23]:
# Dark Data classification
dark_data = {
    'Tree indicators (health, diversity)': {
        'type': 'DD-Type 2 (Data We Know Are Missing)',
        'reason': 'Some tracts have no street trees (parks, industrial, water)',
        'handling': 'tree_count/density filled with 0; diversity left NaN for tracts with <5 trees',
    },
    'Building indicators': {
        'type': 'DD-Type 2 (Data We Know Are Missing)',
        'reason': 'Water-body or park-only tracts with no buildings',
        'handling': 'Left as NaN (structurally absent)',
    },
    'park_area_pct': {
        'type': 'DD-Type 2 (Data We Know Are Missing)',
        'reason': 'Tracts without any park polygon',
        'handling': 'Filled with 0 (no park present)',
    },
    'median_income / ACS variables': {
        'type': 'DD-Type 3 (Choosing Not to Record)',
        'reason': 'Census suppresses income for tracts with very small populations',
        'handling': 'Retain as NaN; exclude from regression (listwise deletion)',
    },
    'SVI RPL_THEMES': {
        'type': 'DD-Type 5 (Measurement Issues)',
        'reason': 'SVI assigns -999 to tracts with insufficient data',
        'handling': 'Recoded -999 as NaN; excluded from vulnerability analyses',
    },
    'Landscape metrics (fragstats)': {
        'type': 'DD-Type 4 (Self-Selection)',
        'reason': 'Tracts outside UrbanWatch raster extent or with <100 valid pixels',
        'handling': 'Left as NaN (typically water bodies)',
    },
}

print('Dark Data Classification:')
print('=' * 80)
for var_group, info in dark_data.items():
    print(f'\n{var_group}')
    print(f'  Type:      {info["type"]}')
    print(f'  Reason:    {info["reason"]}')
    print(f'  Handling:  {info["handling"]}')

Dark Data Classification:
================================================================================

Tree indicators (health, diversity)
  Type:      DD-Type 2 (Data We Know Are Missing)
  Reason:    Some tracts have no street trees (parks, industrial, water)
  Handling:  tree_count/density filled with 0; diversity left NaN for tracts with <5 trees

Building indicators
  Type:      DD-Type 2 (Data We Know Are Missing)
  Reason:    Water-body or park-only tracts with no buildings
  Handling:  Left as NaN (structurally absent)

park_area_pct
  Type:      DD-Type 2 (Data We Know Are Missing)
  Reason:    Tracts without any park polygon
  Handling:  Filled with 0 (no park present)

median_income / ACS variables
  Type:      DD-Type 3 (Choosing Not to Record)
  Reason:    Census suppresses income for tracts with very small populations
  Handling:  Retain as NaN; exclude from regression (listwise deletion)

SVI RPL_THEMES
  Type:      DD-Type 5 (Measurement Issues)
  Reason:    SVI assigns -999 to tracts with insufficient data
  Handling:  Recoded -999 as NaN; excluded from vulnerability analyses

Landscape metrics (fragstats)
  Type:      DD-Type 4 (Self-Selection)
  Reason:    Tracts outside UrbanWatch raster extent or with <100 valid pixels
  Handling:  Left as NaN (typically water bodies)
In [24]:
# Missingness visualization
key_vars = ['tree_count', 'health_index', 'species_diversity', 'mean_dbh',
            'building_count', 'mean_height_m', 'building_coverage',
            'pland_tree_canopy', 'pland_building', 'pland_impervious',
            'mean_far', 'park_area_pct',
            'median_income', 'pct_poverty', 'pct_minority', 'total_pop',
            'RPL_THEMES', 'shdi', 'tree_np', 'bldg_np']
available_vars = [v for v in key_vars if v in master.columns]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
msno.matrix(master[available_vars].reset_index(drop=True), ax=ax, fontsize=8, sparkline=False)
ax.set_title('Missing Data Matrix - Key Indicator Variables', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(VIS + 'missing_data_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print('Saved missing_data_matrix.png')

Saved missing_data_matrix.png

11. Initial Results & Visualizations¶

In [25]:
# Reload trees for borough-level charts
trees_clean = pd.read_csv(PROCESSED + 'trees_cleaned.csv', low_memory=False)
# Fix borough column from spatial join
if 'borough' not in trees_clean.columns:
    if 'borough_right' in trees_clean.columns:
        trees_clean = trees_clean.rename(columns={'borough_right': 'borough'})
    elif 'borough_left' in trees_clean.columns:
        trees_clean = trees_clean.rename(columns={'borough_left': 'borough'})
print(f'Trees for plotting: {len(trees_clean):,}')

Trees for plotting: 652,045

Figure 1: Tree Health Index by Census Tract (Choropleth Map)¶

In [26]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(12, 14))
master_geo = gpd.GeoDataFrame(master, geometry='geometry', crs=tracts.crs)
master_geo.plot(ax=ax, color='#f0f0f0', edgecolor='#cccccc', linewidth=0.2)
has_health = master_geo[master_geo['health_index'].notna()]
has_health.plot(
    column='health_index', ax=ax, cmap='RdYlGn',
    edgecolor='#999999', linewidth=0.15, legend=True,
    legend_kwds={'label': 'Tree Health Index (1=Poor, 3=Good)',
                 'shrink': 0.6, 'orientation': 'horizontal', 'pad': 0.02}
)
ax.set_title('Street Tree Health Index by Census Tract\n'
             'New York City, 2015 TreesCount! Census', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_axis_off()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(VIS + 'map_tree_health_index.png', dpi=200, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print('Saved map_tree_health_index.png')

Saved map_tree_health_index.png

Figure 2: Tree Health Distribution by Borough¶

In [27]:
health_boro = trees_clean.groupby(['borough', 'health']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
health_boro_pct = health_boro.div(health_boro.sum(axis=1), axis=0) * 100
health_boro_pct = health_boro_pct[['Good', 'Fair', 'Poor']]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
health_boro_pct.plot(kind='bar', stacked=True, ax=ax,
                     color=['#2ca02c', '#ffbf00', '#d62728'], edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.5)
ax.set_ylabel('Proportion (%)')
ax.set_xlabel('')
ax.set_title('Street Tree Health Distribution by Borough', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(title='Health Status', bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1), loc='upper left')
ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=0)
for i, boro in enumerate(health_boro_pct.index):
    total = health_boro.loc[boro].sum()
    ax.text(i, 102, f'n={total:,}', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='gray')
ax.set_ylim(0, 112)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(VIS + 'chart_health_by_borough.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print('Saved chart_health_by_borough.png')

Saved chart_health_by_borough.png

Figure 3: Top 15 Street Tree Species¶

In [28]:
native_species = {
    'London planetree': False, 'honeylocust': True, 'pin oak': True, 'red maple': True,
    'Norway maple': False, 'green ash': True, 'American linden': True, 'silver maple': True,
    'sweetgum': True, 'ginkgo': False, 'cherry': False, 'Callery pear': False,
    'littleleaf linden': False, 'Japanese zelkova': False, 'Sophora': False,
}
top15 = trees_clean['spc_common'].value_counts().head(15)
colors = ['#2ca02c' if native_species.get(sp, False) else '#1f77b4' for sp in top15.index]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
ax.barh(range(len(top15)), top15.values, color=colors, edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.5)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(top15)))
ax.set_yticklabels(top15.index, fontsize=9)
ax.invert_yaxis()
ax.set_xlabel('Count')
ax.set_title('Top 15 Street Tree Species in NYC\n(Green = Native to Eastern US)', fontweight='bold')
total = len(trees_clean)
for i, (sp, cnt) in enumerate(top15.items()):
    ax.text(cnt + total * 0.005, i, f'{cnt/total*100:.1f}%', va='center', fontsize=8, color='gray')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(VIS + 'chart_top15_species.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print('Saved chart_top15_species.png')

Saved chart_top15_species.png

Figure 4: Tree Density vs. Median Household Income¶

In [29]:
plot_df = master.dropna(subset=['tree_density', 'median_income'])
plot_df = plot_df[plot_df['median_income'] > 0]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
for boro, color in BOROUGH_COLORS.items():
    subset = plot_df[plot_df['borough'] == boro]
    ax.scatter(subset['median_income'], subset['tree_density'],
              alpha=0.4, s=15, c=color, label=boro, edgecolors='none')
valid = plot_df[['median_income', 'tree_density']].dropna()
r, p = pearsonr(valid['median_income'], valid['tree_density'])
ptext = 'p < 0.001' if p < 0.001 else f'p = {p:.3f}'
ax.text(0.02, 0.98, f'r = {r:.3f}, {ptext}', transform=ax.transAxes, va='top',
        fontsize=9, bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='wheat', alpha=0.5))
ax.set_xlabel('Median Household Income (USD)')
ax.set_ylabel('Tree Density (trees per sq km)')
ax.set_title('Street Tree Density vs. Median Household Income by Census Tract', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(title='Borough', markerscale=2)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(VIS + 'chart_density_vs_income.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print('Saved chart_density_vs_income.png')

Saved chart_density_vs_income.png

Figure 5: UrbanWatch Land Cover Composition by Borough¶

In [30]:
lc_cols = ['pland_building', 'pland_road', 'pland_parking',
           'pland_tree_canopy', 'pland_grass_shrub', 'pland_water']
lc_labels = ['Building', 'Road', 'Parking', 'Tree Canopy', 'Grass/Shrub', 'Water']
lc_colors = ['#d62728', '#7f7f7f', '#bcbd22', '#2ca02c', '#98df8a', '#1f77b4']

lc_by_boro = master.groupby('borough')[lc_cols].mean()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
lc_by_boro.plot(kind='bar', stacked=True, ax=ax, color=lc_colors, edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.5)
ax.set_ylabel('Mean Land Cover Proportion (%)')
ax.set_xlabel('')
ax.set_title('UrbanWatch Land Cover Composition by Borough (1m resolution)', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(labels=lc_labels, title='Land Cover', bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1), loc='upper left')
ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=0)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(VIS + 'chart_landcover_by_borough.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print('Saved chart_landcover_by_borough.png')

Saved chart_landcover_by_borough.png

Figure 6: Building Height vs. Tree Health Index¶

In [31]:
plot_df2 = master.dropna(subset=['mean_height_m', 'health_index'])

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
for boro, color in BOROUGH_COLORS.items():
    subset = plot_df2[plot_df2['borough'] == boro]
    ax.scatter(subset['mean_height_m'], subset['health_index'],
              alpha=0.4, s=15, c=color, label=boro, edgecolors='none')
valid2 = plot_df2[['mean_height_m', 'health_index']].dropna()
r2, p2 = pearsonr(valid2['mean_height_m'], valid2['health_index'])
ptext2 = 'p < 0.001' if p2 < 0.001 else f'p = {p2:.3f}'
ax.text(0.02, 0.98, f'r = {r2:.3f}, {ptext2}', transform=ax.transAxes, va='top',
        fontsize=9, bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='wheat', alpha=0.5))
ax.set_xlabel('Mean Building Height (m)')
ax.set_ylabel('Tree Health Index (1=Poor, 3=Good)')
ax.set_title('Mean Building Height vs. Tree Health Index by Census Tract', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(title='Borough', markerscale=2)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(VIS + 'chart_bldgheight_vs_health.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print('Saved chart_bldgheight_vs_health.png')

Saved chart_bldgheight_vs_health.png

12. Summary Statistics¶

In [32]:
print('=' * 70)
print('SUMMARY STATISTICS')
print('=' * 70)
print(f'\nSpatial units: {len(master)} Census Tracts across 5 boroughs')
print(f'Total alive street trees: {int(master["tree_count"].sum()):,}')
print(f'Mean tree density: {master["tree_density"].mean():.0f} trees/km2 '
      f'(median: {master["tree_density"].median():.0f})')
print(f'Mean Tree Health Index: {master["health_index"].mean():.2f} '
      f'(range: {master["health_index"].min():.2f} - {master["health_index"].max():.2f})')
print(f'Mean species diversity (Shannon): {master["species_diversity"].mean():.2f}')

print('\nBy Borough:')
boro_summary = master.groupby('borough').agg(
    tracts=('GEOID', 'count'),
    trees=('tree_count', 'sum'),
    density=('tree_density', 'mean'),
    health=('health_index', 'mean'),
    diversity=('species_diversity', 'mean'),
    income=('median_income', 'median'),
    tree_canopy_pct=('pland_tree_canopy', 'mean'),
    impervious_pct=('pland_impervious', 'mean'),
).round(1)
print(boro_summary.to_string())

======================================================================
SUMMARY STATISTICS
======================================================================

Total alive street trees: 652,045
Mean tree density: 1093 trees/km2 (median: 1114)
Mean Tree Health Index: 2.76 (range: 1.25 - 3.00)
Mean species diversity (Shannon): 2.63

In [33]:
print('\n--- Notebook complete ---')
print(f'\nProcessed files in data/processed/:')
for f in sorted(os.listdir(PROCESSED)):
    fpath = os.path.join(PROCESSED, f)
    size = os.path.getsize(fpath)
    if size < 1e6:
        print(f'  {f:<45s} {size/1024:>8.0f} KB')
    else:
        print(f'  {f:<45s} {size/1e6:>8.1f} MB')

print(f'\nVisualization files in visualizations/:')
for f in sorted(os.listdir(VIS)):
    print(f'  {f}')
